zh.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III是William Gerard II和de Over Bradelegh的兒子，於1282年之前出生在英國爾金斯利。他於大約1312年與Lady Matilda de Byron在英國英格蘭爾金斯利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1352年在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III是William Gerard II和de Over Bradelegh的儿子，于1282年之前出生在英国爾金斯利。他于大约1312年与Lady Matilda de Byron在英国英格兰爾金斯利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1352年在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Enzo, Gaspare, Luigi, Rocco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Filippo, Francesco, Giovanni, Libero.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the ancient Germanic personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ). This surname is also found in southern France.
German and French (mainly Nord): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name German Gerhard , French Gérard (see Gerard ), using the genitive of the Latinized form Gerardus.

variants.name: Garrison
meanings: English: from a shortened form of Middle English Gerardson, a patronymic from the personal name Gerard + son, or an Anglicized form of its Dutch cognate Gerritsen . The English surname is probably in most cases of Flemish or Dutch origin. It was brought to Britain by immigrants from the Low Countries, especially in the 15th and 16th centuries.
Americanized form of Jewish   Gerson   (see   Gershon  ).
Americanized form of Dutch   Gerritsen  .

variants.name: Garrard
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Gerard  .
Altered form of French Gérard (see Gerard ).
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Geenen
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Geen, a pet form of Gerard .

variants.name: Gerardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Alberto, Alfredo, Aurelio, Carlos, Jesus, Juan, Norberto, Ramon, Tomas, Ana.  Italian Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Enrico, Marco, Marco Antonio.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: English: variant of   Gerard  .

variants.name: Geron
meanings: French (Géron): from the personal name Gero, Gerone, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, as for example Gérard (see Gerard ).
Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
English (Lancashire): of Norman origin, from the personal name Geron of ancient Germanic origin, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gherardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Alessandro, Angelo, Antonio, Donato, Egidio, Ennio, Enzo, Gildo, Lucio, Luigi, Primo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Gherardo, one of themany Italian equivalents of Gerard .

variants.name: Garard
meanings: English (London and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Gerard  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III was born before 1282, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England as the son of William Gerard II and de Over Bradelegh. He married Lady Matilda de Byron about 1312, in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died about 1352, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III est né avant 1282, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, fils de William Gerard II et de Over Bradelegh. Il a épousé Lady Matilda de Byron environ 1312, à Kingsley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé environ 1352, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III wurde vor 1282 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, als Sohn von William Gerard II und de Over Bradelegh geboren. Er hat um 1312 in Kingsley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Lady Matilda de Byron geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb um 1352 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III è nato il prima del 1282 nel Kingsley, Cheshire, England come figlio di William Gerard II e de Over Bradelegh. Lui sposato Lady Matilda de Byron circa 1312, nel Kingsley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì circa 1352, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard IIIは1282年以前にイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーでWilliam Gerard IIとde Over Bradeleghの息子として生まれた。約1312年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアキングスリーでLady Matilda de Byronと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は約1352年に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III(은)는 1282년 이전 영국 체실르 킨그슬리에서 William Gerard II(와)과 de Over Bradelegh의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 킨그슬리에서 Lady Matilda de Byron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1352년 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III nasceu antes 1282, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra filho de William Gerard II e de Over Bradelegh. Ele casou-se com Lady Matilda de Byron aproximadamente 1312, em Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1352, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir William Gerard III родился перед 1282 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия. Его родителей звали William Gerard II и de Over Bradelegh. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Matilda de Byron около 1312 г. в местности кингсли, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер около 1352 г. в его родном городе.

Se casó con Lady Matilda de Byron aproximadamente 1312, en Kingsley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1352, en su pueblo.
